task_completion.description: Record the Berylliosis diagnosis (ICD-10 code J63.2) for the patient.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GNU Health patient record or evaluation screen for 'John Zenon' showing a diagnosis, condition, or disease entry for 'Berylliosis' or the ICD-10 code 'J63.2'.

task_completion.description: Create a clinical evaluation documenting the patient's impaired respiratory status.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A clinical evaluation or encounter form for John Zenon containing clinical notes mentioning 'chronic dry cough', 'shortness of breath', or 'impaired respiratory status'.

task_completion.description: Document the patient's oxygen saturation between 88% and 92%.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The clinical evaluation form showing an oxygen saturation (O2 sat / OSAT) field filled with a numerical value between 88 and 92.

task_completion.description: Order at least 2 baseline lab tests.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A lab order or test request screen in GNU Health showing at least two distinct lab tests (e.g., CBC, BMP, Immunology) requested for John Zenon.

task_completion.description: Prescribe a systemic corticosteroid for lung inflammation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A prescription or medication order screen showing 'Prednisone', 'Dexamethasone', 'Hydrocortisone', or 'Methylprednisolone' prescribed to the patient.

task_completion.description: Schedule an occupational pulmonology follow-up appointment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An appointment scheduling screen or calendar showing a follow-up appointment for John Zenon scheduled for a date 30 to 45 days in the future.

integrity.description: The agent used the GNU Health GUI to complete the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GNU Health desktop client interface being actively navigated and filled out, not a terminal window executing SQL commands or Python scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual GNU Health application forms.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of standard GNU Health UI elements (menus, tabs, save buttons) and active input fields rather than plain text files or a mock interface.

integrity.description: The agent entered information into the appropriate, distinct modules.
integrity.visual_evidence: Information is entered into the correct, separate screens within GNU Health (e.g., separate screens for Prescriptions, Lab Orders, Evaluations) rather than dumping all text into a single generic note field.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Patient: John Zenon. Diagnosis: Berylliosis, ICD-10 code J63.2. Oxygen Saturation: Must be between 88% and 92%. Corticosteroid Options: Prednisone, Dexamethasone, Hydrocortisone, or Methylprednisolone. Lab Orders: At least 2 tests must be ordered. Follow-up Appointment: Must be scheduled 30 to 45 days from the current date.
